reasoning: Hikaru Nakamura's Elo on Wikipedia around November 9, 2025, is 2813. A 1% increase requires gaining approximately 28 Elo points, bringing him to around 2841. In top-level classical chess, gaining 28 points at a 2800+ level requires overwhelmingly dominant performances against other super-GMs, which is exceptionally rare (base rate ~0.007). Moreover, Nakamura is currently playing 'farm' tournaments against significantly lower-rated players solely to meet the 40-game quota for Candidates qualification. In these events, wins offer negligible rating gains (less than 1 point per win), while draws or losses result in significant drops (e.g., losing 4+ points for a draw against an IM, which he explicitly admitted doing recently). Because of this, it is practically mathematically impossible for him to net +28 points by February 2026. Furthermore, for the first resolution date (2025-11-16), FIDE rankings on Wikipedia only update monthly, meaning the rating on Nov 16 will be identical to Nov 9. The probabilities are kept extremely low, increasing marginally for distant horizons to reflect standard uncertainty.
